subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Boards
- **1 main game board** — Four location areas: Artist Colony, Sales Office, Media Center, International Market. Plus the gallery display area.
- **4 player boards** — Personal gallery boards with display walls, office area, and visitor lobby.

#### Art and Artists
- **Artist tiles** — Represent emerging and established artists. Each artist produces art in a specific style (digital, sculpture, painting, photography).
- **Art tiles** — Individual artworks created by artists. Different values based on artist fame.
- **Fame track** — Tracks each artist's fame level, which determines art value.

#### Other Components
- **Visitor meeples** — White (collectors), pink (VIPs). Drawn from bags and placed in galleries.
- **Influence tokens** — Used to acquire art and interact with the market.
- **Money** — Currency for transactions.
- **Reputation tiles** — Track your gallery's reputation.
- **Contract tiles** — Commissions for specific art types.
- **Ticket tokens** — Used for gallery visits.
- **Assistant meeples** — Placed to trigger the kicked-out-action mechanism.

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Main Board Setup
1. **Place the main board** — Four locations clearly visible.
2. **Artist Colony** — Place starting artist tiles in the colony. These are discoverable artists.
3. **International Market** — Set up the market display with art pieces available for purchase.
4. **Media Center** — Place influence and reputation tokens.
5. **Sales Office** — Prepare contract tiles and sales mechanisms.
6. **Visitor bags** — Fill bags with white (collector) and pink (VIP) visitor meeples.

#### Fame Track
- **Starting fame** — All artists begin at base fame. Fame increases through promotion and exhibitions.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Personal Gallery
- **Gallery board** — Each player takes a personal gallery board.
- **Display walls** — Empty at start. You will hang art here.
- **Visitor lobby** — Where visitors wait before entering your gallery.
- **Office** — Tracks administrative capacity.

#### Starting Resources
- **Money** — Starting amount based on turn order.
- **Assistants** — Each player starts with a set of assistant meeples.
- **Influence** — Small starting amount of influence tokens.

#### Worker Placement
- **Gallerist pawn** — Each player has 1 gallerist pawn placed on the board to take actions.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
#### Core Concept
The Gallerist puts you in charge of an art gallery. You discover new artists, buy their artwork, display it to attract visitors, promote artists to increase artwork value, and sell pieces for profit. The game features a unique "kicked-out action" mechanism: when another player moves to a location where you have your gallerist, you get a free bonus action.

#### Game Arc
- **Open-ended rounds** — No fixed round structure. Players take turns until end-game is triggered.
- **Artist fame** — Promoting artists increases the value of their artwork. Multiple players may have art by the same artist.
- **Visitor management** — Attracting collectors and VIPs to your gallery is essential for selling art.
- **Multiple scoring paths** — Art sales, exhibitions, reputation, influence, and visitors all contribute to scoring.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Move your gallerist** — Move your gallerist pawn to one of the 4 locations on the main board.
2. **Kicked-out check** — If another player's gallerist was at that location, they are "kicked out" and immediately get a free bonus action (the kicked-out action).
3. **Perform location action** — Execute one of the actions available at your new location.
4. **Executive action** — You may optionally perform one additional executive action using an assistant.

#### The Four Locations
- **Artist Colony** — Discover new artists and acquire their first artworks.
- **Sales Office** — Sell artwork from your gallery. Requires visitors in your gallery.
- **Media Center** — Promote artists (increase fame), gain influence, improve reputation.
- **International Market** — Buy art from the international market or sell art internationally.

#### Kicked-Out Action
- **Trigger** — When someone moves to your location, you are displaced.
- **Bonus** — You perform a limited free action (one of several options: gain money, move a visitor, gain influence).
- **Strategic placement** — Positioning your gallerist where opponents want to go gives you free bonus actions.
- **Assistants** — You can leave an assistant at a location when you leave, potentially gaining kicked-out bonuses.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Artist Colony Actions
- **Discover artist** — Take an artist tile from the colony. You become their initial patron.
- **Acquire first artwork** — When you discover an artist, you gain their debut artwork.
- **Commission artwork** — Pay an artist you've discovered to create additional pieces.

#### Sales Office Actions
- **Sell art** — Sell a piece from your gallery display. The sale price depends on artist fame + visitor type present.
- **Collector visitors** — White visitors buy art at standard prices.
- **VIP visitors** — Pink visitors buy art at premium prices and provide bonus effects.
- **Gallery requirement** — You must have visitors in your gallery to make sales.

#### Media Center Actions
- **Promote artist** — Increase an artist's fame on the fame track. This raises the value of ALL their artwork (even pieces other players own).
- **Gain influence** — Take influence tokens for use in the international market or other actions.
- **Improve reputation** — Advance your gallery's reputation track for end-game scoring.

#### International Market Actions
- **Buy art** — Purchase artwork from the international market display using money and influence.
- **Sell internationally** — Sell artwork to international buyers for guaranteed prices.
- **Market fluctuation** — Prices in the international market shift based on artist fame.

#### Gallery Management
- **Hang art** — Place purchased artwork on your gallery display walls.
- **Attract visitors** — Visitors are drawn from bags. Art on display attracts specific visitor types.
- **Gallery capacity** — Your gallery has limited wall space and visitor capacity.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Artist Fame
- **Fame track** — Each artist has a position on the fame track (0-20+).
- **Increasing fame** — Promote actions at the Media Center raise fame.
- **Art value** — Artwork value is directly tied to the artist's current fame level.
- **Shared benefit** — If you promote an artist, ALL players holding that artist's work benefit from higher value.
- **Strategic promotion** — Promote artists whose work you hold. Timing matters — promote before selling.

#### Visitors
- **Two types** — Collectors (white) and VIPs (pink).
- **Drawing visitors** — Visitors are drawn randomly from bags based on your actions and art display.
- **Gallery placement** — Visitors go to your gallery lobby first, then must be moved to display rooms.
- **Selling requirement** — At least one visitor must be in your gallery to make a sale.

#### Assistants
- **Placement** — Leave assistants at locations when your gallerist departs.
- **Kicked-out extension** — Assistants at a location also benefit from kicked-out actions.
- **Executive actions** — Spend assistants for additional actions on your turn.
- **Limited supply** — You have a fixed number of assistants. Managing them is critical.

#### Contracts
- **Commissions** — Take contracts requiring specific art types for bonus VP.
- **Fulfillment** — Display the required art in your gallery to complete contracts.
- **Bonuses** — Completed contracts provide VP and sometimes money or influence.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### End-Game Trigger
- **Two conditions** — The game can end when either: (1) a certain number of artists reach maximum fame, or (2) the visitor bags are depleted.
- **Final turns** — After the trigger, each player gets one more turn.

#### Final Scoring
- **Money** — Remaining money converts to VP.
- **Art collection** — VP for artwork still in your gallery (based on artist fame).
- **Sold art** — VP already earned from sales during the game.
- **Visitors** — VP for visitors remaining in your gallery.
- **Reputation** — VP based on reputation track position.
- **Influence** — Remaining influence converts to VP.
- **Contracts** — VP from completed contracts.
- **Bonus tiles** — Various bonus scoring tiles earned during play.

#### Winner
- **Highest VP** — Player with the most victory points wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with more art pieces remaining.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Discover artists early** — Getting first access to an artist's work is valuable. Their art appreciates as fame grows.
- **Promote before selling** — Always try to raise an artist's fame before selling their work for maximum profit.
- **Use the kicked-out mechanism** — Position your gallerist where opponents want to go. Free actions are extremely valuable.
- **Attract visitors steadily** — Without visitors, you cannot sell. Keep your gallery populated.
- **Don't ignore the international market** — It offers guaranteed sales and access to art you didn't discover.
- **Manage assistants carefully** — Running out of assistants limits your flexibility significantly.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
#### Strategy 1: Fame Engine
- **Goal** — Discover artists and aggressively promote them to maximize art value.
- **Key actions** — Artist Colony for discovery, Media Center for promotion, then Sales Office.
- **Benefit** — High-fame art sells for enormous prices.

#### Strategy 2: High-Volume Sales
- **Goal** — Acquire many artworks and sell them quickly, even at moderate prices.
- **Key actions** — Frequent Artist Colony and International Market visits, rapid gallery turnover.
- **Benefit** — Volume compensates for lower per-piece value.

#### Strategy 3: VIP Collector
- **Goal** — Attract VIP visitors for premium sales and special bonuses.
- **Key actions** — Display high-quality art, use media influence to draw VIPs.
- **Benefit** — VIP sales are worth significantly more than standard sales.

#### General Principles
- **Kicked-out awareness** — Always consider where opponents will move. Position yourself for free actions.
- **Shared artist investment** — Promoting an artist helps all holders. If you have the most art, you benefit most.
- **End-game timing** — Watch the end-game triggers. Don't get caught with unsold inventory when the game ends.
